raw_responses.BFI-10_1: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams has shown a preference for smaller, intimate settings. He values the support of close friends and family over large groups. He also mentioned that he feels more comfortable in one-on-one conversations with kids at the nonprofit than in larger group settings. Therefore, it's likely that he would choose a lower number on this scale.
raw_responses.BFI-10_2: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams has shown a tendency to be open and trusting, especially with those who have shown him kindness and support. He mentions trusting his sister, Marcus, and Mr. Thompson, and even gives the benefit of the doubt to his former friends who led him astray. He also seems to trust that people are generally good, as seen in his desire to help others. Therefore, a score of 5 best represents his self-perception as a generally trusting person.
raw_responses.BFI-10_3: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams demonstrates a strong sense of responsibility, proactivity, and a constant drive to improve. He maintains a structured daily routine, works part-time at a nonprofit, and is actively pursuing his GED. He also expresses a desire to better himself and help others. Therefore, it's unlikely that he would describe himself as lazy.
raw_responses.BFI-10_4: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams demonstrates a strong sense of self-awareness and resilience. He acknowledges his past struggles with anxiety and depression but emphasizes his progress in managing stress through self-care and support from loved ones. He also expresses a desire to be a positive role model for his sister, indicating a sense of responsibility and calm under pressure. Therefore, a score of 4 seems most fitting, as it suggests he is generally relaxed and handles stress well, but still recognizes the importance of self-care and support.
raw_responses.BFI-10_5: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams doesn't mention any artistic interests or hobbies. He's more focused on his work at the nonprofit, his GED classes, and being there for his sister. He doesn't seem to have time or interest in artistic pursuits.
raw_responses.BFI-10_6: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams shows a mix of introverted and extroverted traits. He values his family and close friends but also mentions feeling overwhelmed by large social events. He's more comfortable in smaller, intimate settings like his family home or the community center. However, he also actively seeks out mentoring roles and enjoys working with kids at the nonprofit, which suggests he has some sociable tendencies. Considering his overall demeanor and the context of his responses, a score of 3 seems most fitting. It indicates he is sociable but also values his alone time and close relationships over large social gatherings.
raw_responses.BFI-10_7: Throughout the interview, Jerome has shown self-awareness and a willingness to take responsibility for his past mistakes. He doesn't come across as someone who tends to blame others or find fault with them. Instead, he focuses on his own growth and the positive impact he wants to have on others. Therefore, a lower score on this scale seems more fitting for him.
raw_responses.BFI-10_8: Throughout the interview, Jerome has shown a strong commitment to his work at the nonprofit, often staying late to finish tasks or going above and beyond what is expected. He also mentioned his attention to detail when it comes to his GED classes and personal goals. Therefore, it's likely that he sees himself as someone who does a thorough job.
raw_responses.BFI-10_9: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams mentions feeling overwhelmed and anxious at times, especially when talking about his past struggles and the pressure of balancing work, school, and family. He also mentions that he's been working on managing his anxiety through therapy and self-care. Therefore, it's likely that he would rate himself as someone who gets nervous easily, leaning more towards the higher end of the scale.
raw_responses.BFI-10_10: Throughout the interview, Jerome Williams has shown a strong ability to empathize with others, imagine their perspectives, and think outside the box when it comes to helping them. For example, he talks about 'imagining the streets themselves as not always the safest' and 'seeing the strength of the people here.' His detailed responses also suggest a strong ability to visualize and recall past events, further indicating an active imagination.

raw_responses.BES-A_5: Throughout the interview, Jerome has shown a tendency to reflect on his past mistakes and how they affected his relationships with others.
